author: @ronniepirtlejr2606
text: I like your skull and antlers made from bicycle parts hanging on the wall at behind you at 1:40.

author: @Edsbar
text: Things are always cool when you make them yourself as you aren't looking at a thing, no you are looking at achievement.  An adapted saying is 'Beauty is in the eye of the maker'.

author: @donaldhanson2908
text:
Just a note, and Rob may have pointed it out too, the nut would be more secure if you pulled it into the countersink from underneath.
That is, put the counter sink on top of the base plate, so the nut pulls against the baseplate.
I'll grant a GoPro is not a heavy load, but super glue is not meant for a lateral loading.  :)
Good simple design though!

author: @NorthernKitty
text:
Great job at getting a perfect fit!!  Another option is to make the encasement just a little larger than the GoPro, then line it with something like velcro loop tape (not the hook side, but the fuzzy side).  The fit can be a little more forgiving, it will help to dampen vibrations that might otherwise affect the microphone and you won't scratch the camera sliding it in and out.  I've also seen versions with a little pressure screw - stick a little padding onto the side of the camera then turn a thumbscrew through the case to pinch the camera.  
Oh!  And speaking of microphones... don't forget to drill a small holes for them!  Not sure which version GoPro you have, but my microphones are on the sides. 😊

author: @jamesharris5158
text:
Wow, massive improvement in Luke's presentation. Fantastic to see the progress and keep up the good work. I'm still looking forward to the day when Luke has his own channel with videos that don't necessarily fit the ethos of this channel. I think he has great potential. 

It feels like Luke really owned this project, where previously it has felt a bit like he was just going through the motions of someone else's project. I feel that's always the key to a great video.

text: what glue did you use!

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I used regular instant bond super glue and made sure I left it overnight. It was incredibly strong in the morning. Cheers mate :)

author: @synchro-dentally1965
text: M6 looks very similar to 1/4-20 but they're not compatible. Which is more common when it comes to mounting cameras?

How do you activate the buttons? 
With a stick or very thin finger?

author: @Nuts-Bolts
text: Why Gopro could not have included the standard  ¼ inch tripod I don’t know. This looks like the perfect solution (I also like the price). For your type of  workshop environment, I think you’d also find a GorillaPod very useful. One can rap the legs around things to achieve unobstructed overhead or high angle shots. I’d be lost without mine. Talking of  unobstructed shots. During the current lock down, if you can find any budding camera enthusiast to hold the camera for you,   s/he will instinctively point out when the work piece becomes hidden from the camera’s view, such as at 3:20 and again at 3:50 and reposition. Single handedly, this is tiresome to check each camera position to avoid this. I would think, this channel has enough of a track record now, to make it easy to find someone local who would to be willing to help out on the video side, just to get more on-the-job-experience and as a show-case for his camera and editing skills. It a’be a win-win for both parties.
